steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content: The goal is to find the quadratic roots and then identify the values of $h$ and $k$ from the radical form of one solution. Use the quadratic formula on $x^2 - 24x + 137 = 0$: $$x=\frac{24\pm\sqrt{(-24)^2-4(1)(137)}}{2}$$ $$x=\frac{24\pm\sqrt{28}}{2}=12\pm\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{28}$$

steps.title: Match the keyed form
steps.content: One solution is $$x=12-\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{28}.$$ Comparing this with $x=h-\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{k}$ gives $h=12$ and $k=28$.
